participants.name: Sam Hecht
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Combine numbers: 5.10 40 (1.74 10-yd split), 28-inch vertical, 8-5 broad jump, 7.75 3-cone, 4.71 shuttle. Stood on combine numbers at pro day and performed positional drills only. Bears had a large contingent of scouts focused on him. Received a gash near his eye during drill work. Top center prospect in the 2026 class. Listed combine measurements used as pro day measurements were not separately reported.

participants.name: VJ Payne
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Combine numbers: 4.40 40-yard dash, 35-inch vertical, 10-7 broad jump at 6-3, 206 lbs with 33 3/4 arms. Likely stood on combine numbers at pro day. Four-year starter, 207 career tackles, two-time All-Big 12 honorable mention. Listed combine measurements used as pro day measurements were not separately reported.

additional_notes: Kansas State's 2026 Pro Day was held on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, at the indoor practice facility in Manhattan, Kansas. The event was held just one week ago and comprehensive pro day results with all participants' drill numbers have not yet been published by any major outlet. The K-State athletics website published a feature story focused on Sam Hecht, and the Chicago Bears reportedly had a large contingent of scouts in attendance with a focus on Hecht. Only two K-State players (Hecht and Payne) were invited to the NFL Combine. Pro Football Network listed 4 total prospects tracked from K-State's pro day, but individual drill results for additional participants (likely including other seniors/departing players) were not found in any published source. Both Hecht and Payne appear to have stood on their combine numbers and focused on positional drills. Additional participants beyond the two combine invitees almost certainly worked out, but their names and results are not available in currently published sources. This data should be updated when complete pro day results are released by K-State athletics or covered by beat reporters.
